# Review: `deepmind/207.lean` ‚Äî Erd≈ës Problem 207

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 207`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `[erdosproblems.com/207](https://www.erdosproblems.com/207)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Steiner triple systems with high girth).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[KSSS22b]` Kwan, Sah, Sawhney, and Simkin (2022).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos207 ... end Erdos207` (lines 36‚Äì68).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos207`.
- [x] No `open` / `open scoped` statements needed; none present.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `IsSteinerTripleSystem` (line 40) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 38‚Äì39).
- [x] Not marked `noncomputable` ‚Äî correct, it is `Prop`-valued and involves no classical choice.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Type name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `IsSteinerTripleSystem`.
- [x] No local notation used (none needed).
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for `IsSteinerTripleSystem`. **(Optional ‚Äî not required, but could be useful to verify the definition behaves as expected.)**

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 57).
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_207` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` (line 56).
- [x] Has a docstring (lines 44‚Äì55).
- [x] Docstring references the source paper: `[KSSS22b]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem is a direct assertion: "For any g ‚â• 2, if n is sufficiently large and n ‚â° 1, 3 (mod 6), there exists a Steiner triple system on n vertices with girth > g." This is **Case 3** ‚Äî a direct assertion, not a yes/no question or find-a-value question.

- [x] The theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper) ‚Äî correct for Case 3.
- [x] Problem has been solved affirmatively, so the statement is `P` (not `¬¨ P`) ‚Äî correct.
- [x] No quantifiers as theorem parameters; all quantifiers are inside the statement body.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` ‚Äî correct: proved by Kwan‚ÄìSah‚ÄìSawhney‚ÄìSimkin [KSSS22b] but not yet formally verified.
- [x] Not `research open`, so the `sorry` in the proof body is consistent (no linter conflict).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (Combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (trivially, only one number).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

AMS 5 (Combinatorics) is appropriate for a problem about Steiner triple systems and hypergraph girth.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line format, lines 65‚Äì66) ‚Äî acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches (problem is `research solved`, not `research open`).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] No variants present ‚Äî not required for this single-part problem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚àÄ`, `‚àÉ`, `‚â§`, `‚äÜ`, `‚àß`, `‚Ñï`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run. **(Manual verification required.)**
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified. **(Manual verification required.)**

---

## Formalization Correctness Notes

The formalization appears faithful to the original problem:

1. **`IsSteinerTripleSystem`** correctly encodes: (a) every edge has exactly 3 vertices, and (b) every pair of distinct vertices lies in exactly one edge (`‚àÉ!`).
2. **Girth condition**: for any `j` edges with `2 ‚â§ j ‚â§ g`, the union of their vertex sets (`S.biUnion id`) has cardinality at least `j + 3`. This correctly captures that any `j` hyperedges span at least `j + 3` vertices.
3. **Sufficiently large**: `‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ, ‚àÄ n, N‚ÇÄ ‚â§ n ‚Üí ...` ‚Äî standard encoding.
4. **Congruence condition**: `n % 6 = 1 ‚à® n % 6 = 3` ‚Äî correctly captures `n ‚â° 1, 3 (mod 6)`.

---

## Verdict

**PASS.** All required checklist items are satisfied. The formalization is clean, well-documented, and faithful to the original problem. The only unchecked items are optional (sanity-check attributes for the definition) and build verification (requires running `lake build`).

    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 207
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/207](https://www.erdosproblems.com/207)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíFor any $g \geq 2$, if $n$ is sufficiently large and $n \equiv 1, 3 \pmod{6}$, then there
    25‚Üíexists a Steiner triple system on $n$ vertices with girth greater than $g$ ‚Äî that is,
    26‚Üía $3$-uniform hypergraph where every pair of vertices is in exactly one edge, and
    27‚Üíany $j$ edges (for $2 \leq j \leq g$) span at least $j + 3$ vertices.
    28‚Üí
    29‚ÜíThe congruence condition $n \equiv 1, 3 \pmod{6}$ is the necessary and sufficient
    30‚Üídivisibility condition for a Steiner triple system on $n$ points to exist.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[KSSS22b] Kwan, M., Sah, A., Sawhney, M., and Simkin, M., _High-girth Steiner
    33‚Üítriple systems_ (2022).
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos207
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- A set of hyperedges forms a Steiner triple system: each edge has exactly $3$
    39‚Üí    vertices, and every pair of distinct vertices appears in exactly one edge. -/
    40‚Üídef IsSteinerTripleSystem {n : ‚Ñï} (edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    41‚Üí  (‚àÄ e ‚àà edges, e.card = 3) ‚àß
    42‚Üí  (‚àÄ u v : Fin n, u ‚âÝ v ‚Üí ‚àÉ! e, e ‚àà edges ‚àß u ‚àà e ‚àß v ‚àà e)
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/--
    45‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 207 [KSSS22b]:
    46‚Üí
    47‚ÜíFor any $g \geq 2$, if $n$ is sufficiently large and $n \equiv 1$ or $3 \pmod{6}$, there exists
    48‚Üía Steiner triple system on $n$ vertices such that any $j$ edges (for $2 \leq j \leq g$)
    49‚Üíspan at least $j + 3$ vertices.
    50‚Üí
    51‚ÜíThe girth condition forbids short "cycles" in the hypergraph: any collection of
    52‚Üí$j$ edges (for $2 \leq j \leq g$) must contain at least $j + 3$ distinct vertices.
    53‚Üí
    54‚ÜíProved by Kwan, Sah, Sawhney, and Simkin [KSSS22b].
    55‚Üí-/
    56‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    57‚Üítheorem erdos_207 :
    58‚Üí    ‚àÄ g : ‚Ñï, 2 ‚â§ g ‚Üí
    59‚Üí      ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, N‚ÇÄ ‚â§ n ‚Üí
    60‚Üí        (n % 6 = 1 ‚à® n % 6 = 3) ‚Üí
    61‚Üí          ‚àÉ edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n)),
    62‚Üí            IsSteinerTripleSystem edges ‚àß
    63‚Üí            ‚àÄ j : ‚Ñï, 2 ‚â§ j ‚Üí j ‚â§ g ‚Üí
    64‚Üí              ‚àÄ S : Finset (Finset (Fin n)), S ‚äÜ edges ‚Üí S.card = j ‚Üí
    65‚Üí                j + 3 ‚â§ (S.biUnion id).card := by
    66‚Üí  sorry
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üíend Erdos207
    69‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 207

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/207](https://www.erdosproblems.com/207)

For any $g \geq 2$, if $n$ is sufficiently large and $n \equiv 1, 3 \pmod{6}$, then there
exists a Steiner triple system on $n$ vertices with girth greater than $g$ ‚Äî that is,
a $3$-uniform hypergraph where every pair of vertices is in exactly one edge, and
any $j$ edges (for $2 \leq j \leq g$) span at least $j + 3$ vertices.

The congruence condition $n \equiv 1, 3 \pmod{6}$ is the necessary and sufficient
divisibility condition for a Steiner triple system on $n$ points to exist.

[KSSS22b] Kwan, M., Sah, A., Sawhney, M., and Simkin, M., _High-girth Steiner
triple systems_ (2022).
-/

namespace Erdos207

/-- A set of hyperedges forms a Steiner triple system: each edge has exactly $3$
    vertices, and every pair of distinct vertices appears in exactly one edge. -/
def IsSteinerTripleSystem {n : ‚Ñï} (edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
  (‚àÄ e ‚àà edges, e.card = 3) ‚àß
  (‚àÄ u v : Fin n, u ‚âÝ v ‚Üí ‚àÉ! e, e ‚àà edges ‚àß u ‚àà e ‚àß v ‚àà e)

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 207 [KSSS22b]:

For any $g \geq 2$, if $n$ is sufficiently large and $n \equiv 1$ or $3 \pmod{6}$, there exists
a Steiner triple system on $n$ vertices such that any $j$ edges (for $2 \leq j \leq g$)
span at least $j + 3$ vertices.

The girth condition forbids short "cycles" in the hypergraph: any collection of
$j$ edges (for $2 \leq j \leq g$) must contain at least $j + 3$ distinct vertices.

Proved by Kwan, Sah, Sawhney, and Simkin [KSSS22b].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_207 :
    ‚àÄ g : ‚Ñï, 2 ‚â§ g ‚Üí
      ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, N‚ÇÄ ‚â§ n ‚Üí
        (n % 6 = 1 ‚à® n % 6 = 3) ‚Üí
          ‚àÉ edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n)),
            IsSteinerTripleSystem edges ‚àß
            ‚àÄ j : ‚Ñï, 2 ‚â§ j ‚Üí j ‚â§ g ‚Üí
              ‚àÄ S : Finset (Finset (Fin n)), S ‚äÜ edges ‚Üí S.card = j ‚Üí
                j + 3 ‚â§ (S.biUnion id).card := by
  sorry

end Erdos207

content.text: The review verdict is **PASS**. No changes needed.
